skill.name: update-docs
skill.description: Reviews project documentation against the current codebase and guides safe updates while preserving decisions, warnings, and historical context.
skill.summary: Synchronize documentation with code while preserving important project knowledge.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many code fence and inline markdown tokens as shell, crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Most are false positives from documentation examples, but the skill does instruct agents to search, read, edit, rename, and run git or build validation commands across a repository. No evidence found of malicious intent, network exfiltration, credential theft, obfuscation, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Broad Repository Read and Edit Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE for operational risk. The skill directs agents to discover documentation files, read code thoroughly, edit documentation, rename plan files, and save update summaries. This is legitimate for a documentation skill, but it can modify many files if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs agents to search, read, edit, rename, and validate repository documentation. The behavior is expected for the skill but has meaningful filesystem impact.

medium_findings.title: Delegation of Full Document Content to Subagent
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE for data exposure within the tool environment. The Oracle prompt asks the agent to send each document path and full content to a general-purpose subagent for analysis. This may expose sensitive repository documentation to another model context, although no external network destination is specified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subagent prompt includes placeholders for document path and full content. The risk depends on platform isolation, but the exposure path is clearly described.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for most listed external command hits. The detected locations are markdown code fences, inline glob patterns, status examples, and prose, not executable shell code. The supporting tools section does mention Bash and git operations, so command use should be reviewed by the executing agent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines are markdown instructions and examples rather than runnable scripts. Only the general instruction to use Bash for git operations remains operationally relevant.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Keyword Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm detections map to words inside the skill description, checklist prose, documentation examples, and table text. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher selection, or executable implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and documentation labels at the flagged lines, not crypto code. There is no evidence of weak cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Documentation Review Instructions
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE as malware reconnaissance. The flagged lines describe checking cross-references, rationale, diagrams, and a mermaid example. These are normal documentation maintenance activities, with no host inventory, credential search, or persistence behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are documentation workflow text and a mermaid diagram fence. They do not instruct operating system enumeration or data collection outside the repository.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository-Wide File Discovery and Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow asks agents to find many documentation paths and update them, including plan file renames and summary file creation. This is not malicious, but it can create broad changes and should be used with source control review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relevant instructions are explicit and central to the skill. The risk is controlled by the user and agent execution environment.

content.value_statement: Project documentation often drifts from the codebase and loses important historical context during cleanup. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through careful review, preservation, updates, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Finds project documentation in .plans, docs, .tasks, CLAUDE.md, and user-specified paths.
Compares documentation claims with current code and project conventions.
Preserves decisions, warnings, historical notes, and non-obvious project knowledge.
Updates plan status files as todo, done, or obsolete after code verification.
Creates a documentation update summary with changed files and coverage gaps.
Checks links, examples, diagrams, and terminology for consistency.
limitations: Requires repository access and careful human review before accepting broad edits.
Cannot prove business intent when documentation conflicts with undocumented code.
May require project builds or link checks that are unavailable in some environments.
Subagent analysis may expose document content inside the AI tool environment.

use_cases.title: Refresh Engineering Docs After a Release
use_cases.description: Review architecture notes, API docs, and examples so they match the shipped code.

use_cases.title: Audit Plans Before Closing Work
use_cases.description: Verify each plan item against code before marking a plan done, todo, or obsolete.

use_cases.title: Preserve Project Knowledge During Cleanup
use_cases.description: Update old documents without deleting decisions, warnings, migration notes, or gotchas.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Documentation Refresh
prompt_templates.prompt: Use update-docs to review the docs folder and update obvious outdated paths, examples, and feature descriptions. Preserve warnings and decision notes.

prompt_templates.title: CLAUDE.md Accuracy Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use update-docs to audit every CLAUDE.md file against the current repository. Keep project conventions, but fix claims that no longer match the code.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Status Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use update-docs to inspect .plans files and verify each checklist item against implementation. Rename plans only when the status is fully confirmed.

prompt_templates.title: Full Documentation Preservation Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use update-docs for a full documentation audit. Run the preservation review first, update docs from code evidence, validate links, and produce a summary of gaps.

output_examples.input: Update docs after the authentication refactor.
output: Preservation checklist for authentication decisions and warnings.
Updated API and setup documentation that matches the refactored code.
Summary of changed files, preserved sections, and remaining coverage gaps.

output_examples.input: Check whether the payment migration plan is done.
output: Verified checklist items with evidence from current implementation.
Plan status recommendation as todo, done, or obsolete.
Notes for any items that require human confirmation.
Updated diagrams that reflect current components and data flow.
List of removed or obsolete diagram claims.
Validation notes for references and examples.

best_practices: Run the workflow on a clean branch so documentation edits are easy to review.
Give the agent the product area or release scope when a full repository audit is not needed.
Review preservation checklists before accepting changes to architecture or decision documents.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to rewrite decision history without human approval.
Do not mark plans done from text alone without checking implementation.
Do not run broad edits on repositories that contain sensitive private documentation unless the AI environment is approved.

faq.question: What does this skill update?
faq.answer: It targets .plans, CLAUDE.md, docs, .tasks, and other documentation paths requested by the user.

faq.question: Does it delete old documentation?
faq.answer: It favors preservation. It warns against deleting decisions, warnings, history, and gotchas without explicit approval.

faq.question: Can it mark plan files as done?
faq.answer: Yes, but only after verifying every plan item against the current codebase.

faq.question: Does it require command execution?
faq.answer: It may use file search, git history, build checks, and link checks when the environment allows them.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow can read broad documentation content and may delegate content to a subagent.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
